title: pkcs11: put returns Err when new value is already committed (destroy failure)
description: Trigger: create_object succeeds but destroy_object on the old handle fails. Function returns Err to caller. Caller has no way to know the new secret IS committed — it is live in the HSM and get() will return it — but they received an error. A retry creates a third duplicate. Correct behavior: return Ok(()) and log the cleanup failure, since the write succeeded. Returning Err here is a lie about whether the put succeeded.
